title: Key-Value Stores Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about key-value stores and their role in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is a 'Key-Value Store' and why is it useful?
options: A way to backup the database.
The simplest and fastest type of NoSQL database, storing data as a unique key associated with a specific value.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which of these is the most popular in-memory key-value store, famously used for caching and sessions?
What's the meaning of 'In-Memory' in a key-value store like Redis?
options: The data is stored on a flash drive.
The data is stored and retrieved entirely from the server's RAM (Memory), providing sub-millisecond response times.

questions.question: Which of these is a typical use case for a key-value store?
options: Caching the results of expensive database queries.

questions.question: What is the primary role of a 'TTL' (Time To Live) in a key-value store?
options: To automatically delete keys after a certain period of time, preventing memory from filling up with stale data.
To automatically generate a backup of the database.
